records.option: (b) Because Mount Sel was an easy hill to climb in the winter.
records.quote: Because the Greywood gave shade, but it was dark and easy to get lost in.

records.option: (b) To lead the long hunts deep into the Greywood.
records.quote: Decorating the boats was the work of the craft guild, not the council.

records.option: (b) Because the Flats had the richest soil for tall crops.
records.quote: The Ashen Flats were dry and bare, and almost nothing would grow there.

records.option: (b) To catch enough fish to feed the whole town.
records.quote: Feeding the town was the fishers' work, not the keeper of the gate.

records.option: (b) Bright amber beads gathered from the river.
records.quote: Clay pots came from the Old Harbor potters, a different people than the Mott.

records.option: (b) To keep their sheep from wandering off into the valley.
records.quote: The wall was about safety, not about keeping sheep in.

records.option: (b) It was a kind of fishing net.
records.quote: A fishing net is only a tool, which this clearly is not.

records.option: (b) To cut down the oldest trees to build new boats.
records.quote: Boat wood was taken from the river groves, not from deep in the Greywood.

records.option: (b) It marked the way to the summer trading market.
records.quote: The market paths were marked with white posts, not by a banner.
The oldest person in the village always became the leader.

records.option: (b) They raced their boats all the way across Lake Onu.
records.quote: Trading happened at the spring market, a separate event from this festival.

records.option: (b) They wanted to live closer to the deep winter snow.
records.quote: They wanted to live closer to the deep winter snow.

records.option: (b) To cook the whole day's catch for the town to share.
records.quote: The fire on the point was lit all year, not only when the season ended.

records.option: (b) It was the shortest way by a full half a day.
records.quote: The cliff road was actually the long way around, not the shortest.
